channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I kept losing the context behind my code, so I built Kote
item.description: I kept running into the same problem: I'd solve an issue or make a technical decision, and a few weeks later I had to search through old AI conversations, Git history, and notes to recover the...

item.title: Orion Belt v1.0 — from Alpha bastion to a stable self-hosted SSH PAM
item.description: Earlier I wrote Meet Orion-Belt, Go ZeroTrust Bastion — the Alpha “here’s the idea” post. This is the follow-up: I tagged v1.0.0, moved the project under orion-belt-dev, and I’m willing to call...

item.title: I thought fallback was a nice-to-have until OpenAI billing issues broke 3 agent runs in one week
item.description: A lot of teams still treat fallback like a polish feature. Something you add after v1. Something for "enterprise reliability" later. I think that mindset is dead if you're building agents that...

item.title: Three scripts that kill freelance scope creep without losing the client
item.description: "Hey, while you're in there, could you also just..." Every freelancer knows the cold dread of that sentence. Scope creep is how a profitable project quietly turns into an unpaid one. The problem...

item.title: Let AI agents use production data without handing them your database
item.description: Hi Devs, If you’ve connected an AI agent to a real database, you’ve probably felt the discomfort of the default approach: handing the model an execute_sql(sql) tool. Read-only roles, SQL...

item.title: How I built a pure Python DNA Genome assembler using B&B
item.description: My attempt at solving the Shortest Common Superstring problem using graph theory and no additional libraries. Overview Assembling DNA Genome is a problem that consists in finding a superstring...

item.title: The LLM Was the Easy Part: Building a Hybrid RAG API
item.description: A basic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) demo is surprisingly small: Embed some documents. Retrieve the closest chunks. Add them to a prompt. Ask an LLM to generate an answer. But when I...

item.title: How to add PostHog to Next.js with GDPR-aware consent
item.description: Implement PostHog in a Next.js App Router project with c15t, then use a GDPR-aware consent setup that waits for measurement consent before loading analytics. Analytics code should match the...

item.title: If a bank hired me to fix their webhook handling, here is how NestJS would help me start
item.description: A bank or payment provider sends a webhook the moment something happens. A transfer completes, a payment settles, a dispute is opened. The backend receiving that webhook is supposed to update its...

item.title: Adaptional (YC S25) Is Hiring
item.description: Article URL: https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/adaptional/jobs Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48937125 Points: 0 # Comments: 0

item.title: Google is better at playing the EU regulations game
item.description: Today, the European Union ordered Google to give its AI rivals greater access to Android, the open-source operating system that powers billions of devices worldwide. The demand is hardly...

item.title: Roblox will let people use AI to make games on their phone
item.description: Roblox is about to let people make games with AI right inside its mobile app, which could make a platform that's already filled with content of questionable quality feel even more overloaded. The...

item.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Embed Ranks #1 Overall on RTEB, Advancing Agentic Retrieval
Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
item.description: With this new update, Google is expanding AI Mode beyond answering questions and into completing tasks across the apps they use regularly.

item.title: Why teens deserve access to safe AI
item.description: Learn how OpenAI is making ChatGPT safer for teens with age-appropriate protections, learning tools, parental controls, and expert partnerships.

item.title: Connect more of your apps to Search
item.description: Connected apps rendering

item.title: Create, edit and star in videos with two Google Vids updates
item.description: Text "Gemini Omni and Personal Avatars in Google Vids" surrounded by various images

item.title: Google is renaming NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook
item.description: Google is giving its AI note-taking app a new name. The company announced on Thursday that NotebookLM is becoming Gemini Notebook, but will remain a standalone app even as it integrates more...

item.title: Energy IPOs surge as investors hunt for ways to play AI boom
item.description: Companies coming to market are raising money at fastest pace this century.

item.title: Time’s running out to save $10 on Splatoon Raiders physical preorders
item.description: Splatoon Raiders’ July 23rd launch day is almost here, so your window to preorder the physical version at a $10 discount is about to close. The digital version is $49.99, and so is the game on...

item.title: ThreatsDay: Game Cheat Spyware, 24-Hour Ransomware, Chrome Sync Stalking + 12 More Stories
item.description: A lot of this week’s trouble starts with something that looks close enough. A familiar repo. A useful installer. A harmless sync setting. Then the handoff goes bad, the box starts talking to...

item.title: Tesla driver in fatal Texas crash overrode FSD by pressing accelerator ‘100 percent,’ investigators confirm
item.description: The Tesla driver who fatally struck a woman after crashing into her home "manually overrode" the vehicle's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology by pressing the gas pedal to 100 percent, the National...

item.title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
item.description: DoorDash is opening a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool that lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal, marking another step toward...

item.title: Why is OpenAI selling a ChatGPT basketball?
item.description: You may have heard that OpenAI released its first piece of hardware this week. You may not have heard about the ChatGPT basketball.

item.title: Apple’s OLED iPad Mini upgrade is on the way as prices continue to rise
item.description: The iPad Mini could get an OLED display upgrade as soon as October, according to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. It would be the most significant refresh for the Mini since its 2021 redesign. An OLED...

item.title: How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product
item.description: Drawing on more than a decade spent helping build some of the world's most influential AI systems, including research that later informed the development of ChatGPT, Andrew Dai explains why he...

item.title: Why AMI Labs’ Alexandre LeBrun won’t call his AI ‘AGI’ or ‘superintelligence’
item.description: While everyone in AI is chasing "superintelligence," Alexandre LeBrun, CEO of Yann LeCun’s world model startup, AMI Labs, dismisses the word.

item.title: Protecting Privacy in an AI Era
item.description: Daniel Solove argues in the Wall Street Journal (alternate link) that giving people control of their personal data is not an effective way to regulate privacy in this era. Instead, we need to hold...

item.title: Moonshot’s upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
item.description: The FT reports Kimi K3 will be the largest open AI model from China, with a parameter count between 2 trillion and 3 trillion.

item.title: ‘No company is going to go to jail for you’: Proton’s CTO on balancing privacy, policy, and trust
item.description: Today on Decoder, we’ve got the first of a two-part series on the systems that run the world: I’m talking with Bart Butler, the CTO of Proton, the company that makes private and secure...
